paper_title: Vertebrate Paleontology and Stratigraphy in the Ica Desert

methods.description: This method involves documenting the physical stratigraphy and architecture of outcrops using conventional geological field methods. It includes sedimentological logging, facies analysis, and geological mapping to understand the stratigraphic framework of the area.

steps.description: Document the physical stratigraphy and architecture of the studied outcrops through conventional geological field methods.
steps.searchable_summary: perform geological field survey to document stratigraphy

steps.description: Conduct high-resolution sedimentological logging of a 40-m-thick section.
steps.searchable_summary: log sedimentological data for a 40-m section

steps.description: Perform facies analysis and geological mapping at 1:10,000 scale.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze facies and map geology at 1:10,000 scale

steps.description: Combine allostratigraphic and lithostratigraphic criteria for stratigraphic analysis.

methods.description: This method involves the survey and preliminary identification of fossils in the field, recording their geographic and stratigraphic positions, and collecting significant specimens for further study.

steps.description: Make taphonomic observations and preliminarily identify each fossil in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field survey for fossil identification

steps.description: Record the geographic position of each vertebrate specimen using a handheld GPS.
steps.searchable_summary: record GPS coordinates for vertebrate fossils

steps.description: Establish the stratigraphic position of each specimen along the measured section with an accuracy of ±0.4 to ±3 m.
Collect significant specimens for deposition at the Museo de Historia Natural de la Universidad Nacional Mayor de San Marcos (MUSM) in Lima.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and prepare significant fossils for museum deposition
